title: caltha palustris f. pygmaea
intro: Caltha palustris f. pygmaea, also known as dwarf marsh marigold, is a compact, perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Caltha palustris f. pygmaea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in topical creams, ointments, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
